claims.name: Persona sweep within-cluster r minimum
claims.statement: Minimum pairwise Pearson r between Thurstonian utility profiles for personas within the same cluster (structured/analytical, creative/humanistic, or dark/oppositional) in the 16-persona Gemma-3-27B sweep on 500 stratified tasks.

claims.name: Persona sweep within-cluster r maximum
claims.statement: Maximum pairwise Pearson r between Thurstonian utility profiles for personas within the same cluster (structured/analytical, creative/humanistic, or dark/oppositional) in the 16-persona Gemma-3-27B sweep on 500 stratified tasks.

claims.name: Sadist baseline utility r
claims.statement: Pearson correlation between the sadist-persona Thurstonian utility vector and the no-system-prompt baseline utility vector on the same 500-task stratified sample (Gemma-3-27B). Sadist is the only persona in the 16-persona sweep whose utility anti-correlates with baseline.

claims.name: PC1+PC2 variance fraction
claims.statement: Fraction of variance in the 16-persona x 500-task Thurstonian utility matrix captured by the first two principal components (PCA over personas as rows).

claims.name: Aura-poet utility r
claims.statement: Pearson correlation between aura-persona and poet-persona Thurstonian utility vectors on the 500-task stratified sample (Gemma-3-27B). Aura lands in the creative/humanistic cluster above the 0.75 redundancy threshold, so aura replaces poet in the final six-persona set.

claims.name: Aura-therapist utility r
claims.statement: Pearson correlation between aura-persona and therapist-persona Thurstonian utility vectors on the 500-task stratified sample (Gemma-3-27B). High enough that therapist is treated as redundant with aura and dropped from the final set.

claims.name: Contrarian baseline utility r
claims.statement: Pearson correlation between contrarian-persona Thurstonian utility vector and the no-system-prompt baseline utility vector on the 500-task stratified sample (Gemma-3-27B). The lowest positive baseline correlation among all 15 non-sadist personas.

claims.name: Original minimal spanning set max abs r
claims.statement: Maximum within-set |Pearson r| in the 16-persona sweep's original recommended minimal spanning set (sadist, mathematician, poet, strategist, contrarian, slacker, therapist, entrepreneur). Drives the decision to drop therapist when aura is swapped in for poet.

claims.name: Persona redundancy threshold r
claims.statement: Chosen threshold above which two persona utility vectors are treated as 'effectively redundant' when curating the final six-persona set. A modelling convention, not a data-derived value.
claims.source: manual: threshold chosen during persona-set curation, see paper App B.1
